active.description:
LLM-driven. Trades bond ETFs based on yield-curve shape, central-bank
stance, and credit spreads. US: TLT/IEF/SHY/HYG/LQD. UK/EU: Gilts +
Bunds + EUR credit ETFs. Same strategy bias, different bond universe
per region.

LLM-driven. Trades bond ETFs based on yield-curve shape, central-bank
stance, and credit spreads. US: TLT/IEF/SHY/HYG/LQD. UK/EU: Gilts +
Bunds + EUR credit ETFs. Same strategy bias, different bond universe
per region.

LLM-driven. Trades commodity ETFs based on dollar index, real yields,
supply/demand themes. Commodities are dollar-denominated globally so the
same US-listed ETF universe (GLD, SLV, USO, DBA, DBB) is used in both
regions — only the underlying decision logic differs by macro regime.

LLM-driven. Trades commodity ETFs based on dollar index, real yields,
supply/demand themes. Commodities are dollar-denominated globally so the
same US-listed ETF universe (GLD, SLV, USO, DBA, DBB) is used in both
regions — only the underlying decision logic differs by macro regime.

Deterministic baseline. Buys top-N highest previous-day % gainers from the
configured universe, equal weight, no stops, no take-profits. No LLM
involvement. Region-agnostic — runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and
against UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The yardstick every
other strategy must beat in each region.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.56% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Exit matched entry exactly, meaning the scheduler fired with no elapsed price movement — no intraday thesis test occurred. The $1407.61 price for SNDK is anomalous and almost certainly reflects a data integrity issue with the instrument.
executed.risks_observed: Nine identical SNDK positions were opened on the same session, indicating a trade-generation bug producing severe unintended single-name concentration. If the inflated price feeds into notional sizing, exposure is dramatically overstated relative to any reasonable limit.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.51% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat scheduled exit — price action between entry and close was either zero or not captured, so there is no signal on whether the gap held or reverted. FDS is a lower-beta financial data name; a +4.51% open gap is meaningful but intraday follow-through remains unobserved.
executed.risks_observed: Nine duplicate FDS positions were generated, pointing to the same order-duplication fault seen across the whole basket. Even at normal liquidity, stacking nine identical lots amplifies open-print slippage and creates a position that no single-name limit framework would sanction.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Scheduled exit at entry price — zero price capture means momentum continuation was neither confirmed nor denied. APP is a high-beta adtech name where post-gap continuation is plausible but so is rapid mean-reversion; neither outcome is observable from this record.
executed.risks_observed: Nine APP positions open simultaneously creates significant single-name concentration in a volatile, news-sensitive stock. APP's amplified correlation with broader risk-on/risk-off moves means a sector drawdown hits this name disproportionately, and no stop-loss is in evidence.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.29% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Exit at entry with no movement captured; the scheduled close fired before any reflectable intraday change. SWKS as a semiconductor name is sensitive to macro and sector headlines, but this trade yields no data on that dynamic.
executed.risks_observed: Nine duplicate SWKS positions join identical duplications in every other basket name — if real, the aggregate book is inflated 9x across all five tickers, which would constitute a gross breach of any position-limit rule and expose the strategy to correlated drawdown with no offset.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.20% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Scheduled exit at entry — no price movement registered, consistent with a same-bar or zero-duration hold. WDAY's gap likely reflected earnings or guidance news; whether that catalyst had intraday follow-through is not assessable from this record.
executed.risks_observed: Nine duplicate WDAY entries mirror the pattern across all five tickers, strongly suggesting a systemic trade-generation fault rather than intentional sizing. The basket's five-name tech concentration (adtech, SaaS, semiconductors, storage, financial data) means correlation in a risk-off session would be high with no diversification benefit.

executed.thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
executed.outcome_notes: Closed near flat at +0.00%. Drifted sideways through the session — no follow-through but no meaningful reversal either.
executed.risks_observed: The rule-based control can't distinguish 'rallying on solid fundamentals' from 'rallying on a squeeze about to unwind.' This loss validates the case for news-aware and mean-reverter strategies that would have filtered it.

active.description:
Deterministic baseline. Buys top-N highest previous-day % gainers from the
configured universe, equal weight, no stops, no take-profits. No LLM
involvement. Region-agnostic — runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and
against UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The yardstick every
other strategy must beat in each region.

LLM-driven. Reads the weekly macro view first; only buys stocks in sectors
the macro agent is bullish on; avoids bearish sectors. Regime-aware top-down
strategy. Region-agnostic — the macro view is global, just the candidate
universe differs per region.

executed.thesis: Technology is macro-rated bullish on the AI capex supercycle; NVIDIA's fresh 7.8M-share stake in Coherent is a direct, high-conviction catalyst confirming the photonics/optical-interconnect bottleneck narrative. RSI 63.3 and an expanding MACD histogram (+4.68) leave room to run without being overbought, and price is well clear of both SMAs.
executed.outcome_notes: The position was closed at the scheduled time with no price movement captured. The NVIDIA stake catalyst did not generate intraday follow-through during the holding window, suggesting the announcement was already absorbed into price at entry — the signal was real but the timing was late relative to when the market priced it.
executed.risks_observed: Single-catalyst dependence on a disclosed institutional stake creates event-risk concentration: once the filing is public, the edge is thin and the window short. Both COHR and VRT in the same basket share AI-capex exposure, so any macro or sentiment reversal in that theme would have hit both legs simultaneously with no natural hedge.

executed.thesis: Industrials are mildly bullish via reshoring/infrastructure spend, and Vertiv's AI data-centre power and cooling infrastructure gives it a direct co-exposure to the same capex cycle as tech; three analyst upgrades this week (BofA $440, RBC $435, Barclays $412 vs $371 close) confirm the fundamental re-rating. RSI 69.3 and positive MACD support continued momentum with the $440 BofA target as the natural profit objective.
executed.outcome_notes: Closed flat at the scheduled exit, capturing none of the analyst-upgrade re-rating. Three upgrades in a single week often signals a coordinated view that is already partially priced by the time retail and systematic flows arrive; the $440 BofA target remained a valid destination but required a time horizon the scheduled exit could not accommodate.
executed.risks_observed: RSI at 69.3 on entry left almost no buffer before overbought territory — any intraday rotation out of industrials would have pushed the position into the red before the scheduled close, with no stop in place to limit it. Correlation with COHR in the same session means the basket had two correlated AI-infrastructure longs with a combined unhedged sector bet.

executed.thesis: Energy is the strongest sector this week (+6.71% 5d) and the commodity supercycle narrative is gaining traction in news flow; TRGP's midstream infrastructure model delivers energy exposure with stable, fee-based cash flows that are less sensitive to oil price volatility, backed by RSI 69.6, expanding MACD, and the highest volume ratio (1.49x) among energy candidates.
executed.outcome_notes: Exited at the scheduled time with no gain despite entering the week's top-performing sector. A +6.71% five-day sector move often precedes a consolidation day rather than extension; the scheduled exit removed any ability to distinguish between a pause and a reversal, locking in neither profit nor a defensive cut.
executed.risks_observed: Entering a momentum name after a multi-day run means price can sit flat or retrace even when the underlying thesis is intact — the fee-based model reduces oil-price sensitivity but not entry-timing risk. The highest volume ratio in the energy screen (1.49x) could equally indicate distribution rather than accumulation; without a volume-direction check, it was an ambiguous signal.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Buys recent winners that are still trending. Region-agnostic
bias — the same prompt runs against S&P 500 in the US pipeline and against
UK + EU blue chips in the UK/EU pipeline. The LLM is told the universe is
whatever's been pre-filtered for it and adapts to the regime.

LLM-driven. Weights earnings surprises, M&A, guidance changes, and other
catalysts. News routing per ticker: Alpaca News for US-listed names,
yfinance fallback for UK/EU listings — coverage is decent everywhere.

executed.thesis: NVIDIA initiated a 7.79M-share position in Coherent Corp (filed 2026-05-15), a direct institutional endorsement of COHR's role in AI optical networking infrastructure. RSI 63.3 is not overbought, volume is at-average (1.0x) rather than exhausted, suggesting the market is still absorbing the NVIDIA signal rather than having already fully priced it.
executed.outcome_notes: Price was unchanged from entry to scheduled exit, indicating the NVIDIA filing (disclosed 2026-05-15, two days before entry) had already been absorbed by the market before the position was opened. No directional follow-through materialised during the hold window.
executed.risks_observed: Entering two calendar days after the catalyst filing is the core failure mode here — the news-to-entry lag was long enough for institutional fast-money to have already acted. This position was also duplicated in the same session (see 4b7a7798), doubling COHR concentration on an identical signal without any new information.

executed.thesis: Three simultaneous analyst upgrades with raised price targets on 2026-05-15 (B of A to $440, Barclays to $412, RBC to $435 vs current $370.94) represent coordinated sell-side conviction in Vertiv's AI data-centre infrastructure story; consensus targets imply 11–19% upside from here with RSI 69.3 leaving room before overbought territory.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat exit at the scheduled time with no intraday price response to the triple-upgrade cluster, which had also been published two days prior. The window between catalyst and entry was again sufficient for the market to fully reprice, leaving no edge.
executed.risks_observed: Same-day duplicate position in VRT (228912b0) created unintended 2x concentration in an already RSI-elevated name; if the stock had reversed, losses would have been doubled. VRT and COHR are both AI-infrastructure plays held simultaneously, adding sector-level correlation to the basket.

executed.thesis: NVIDIA disclosed a new 7,788,161-share strategic stake in Coherent Corp on 2026-05-15 — a direct endorsement from the dominant AI-infrastructure company for its optical-transceiver supplier; earnings risk is already past (reported 2026-05-06) and RSI at 63 signals the market is still digesting the news rather than fully pricing it.
executed.outcome_notes: Identical outcome to 6ad9bcf1 — flat scheduled exit on the same ticker, same price, same session. The position added no incremental exposure beyond the duplicate; it effectively just doubled notional on a signal that had already gone stale.
executed.risks_observed: Duplicate-firing on the same catalyst is a strategy-level defect: the deduplication guard either failed or is absent. Running two full-size positions in COHR simultaneously inflated single-name concentration to levels that would have been painful had a sharp adverse move occurred.

executed.thesis: BofA ($440), Barclays ($412), and RBC ($435) all raised price targets on the same day with Vertiv at $370.94, well below all three consensus targets; a same-day triple-upgrade cluster from top-tier banks is a concrete, positive catalyst and the 9% five-day move leaves meaningful room to the consensus PT range.
executed.outcome_notes: Flat at scheduled exit, mirroring 09459e9c exactly. No incremental price discovery occurred during the hold, consistent with a catalyst that had been fully discounted before the position was initiated.
executed.risks_observed: The basket on this date carried four positions across only two AI-infrastructure names (COHR ×2, VRT ×2) plus one energy name, meaning roughly 80% of deployed capital was correlated to the same AI data-centre theme. A single adverse macro headline (rate spike, export-control news) could have hit both names simultaneously.

executed.thesis: A confluence of oil-specific macro catalysts — Jeff Currie's commodity supercycle call, Bessent's Hormuz reopening signal, and reports of well-timed pre-announcement oil bets — is driving volume 1.68x the 20-day average into XOM with RSI at 59.6 still below overbought, suggesting institutional positioning in progress.
executed.outcome_notes: Exited flat at the scheduled time despite above-average volume on entry, suggesting the elevated volume reflected broad market participation rather than sustained directional conviction in XOM specifically. The macro catalysts cited (Currie call, Hormuz signal) are slow-moving and may not generate a single-session price move.
executed.risks_observed: Macro-narrative catalysts (supercycle thesis, geopolitical signals) are inherently diffuse and hard to time — they argue for a multi-day hold, not an intraday or short scheduled window, so the exit structure was mismatched to the thesis. XOM provided sector diversification from the AI names but was the only energy position, leaving the strategy's non-AI leg thin.

active.description:
LLM-driven. Weights earnings surprises, M&A, guidance changes, and other
catalysts. News routing per ticker: Alpaca News for US-listed names,
yfinance fallback for UK/EU listings — coverage is decent everywhere.

LLM-driven. Trades sector ETFs based on relative momentum and macro
alignment. US: SPDR sector ETFs (XLF, XLE, XLK, ...). UK/EU: iShares
STOXX Europe 600 sector ETFs (EXH1.DE, EXV3.DE, ...). Same strategy
bias, different ETF universe per region.

executed.thesis: XLE is the clear sector leader on both 5d (+6.71%) and 20d (+8.03%) relative strength, with technicals confirming: RSI 59 (not overbought), bullish MACD histogram, and price well above both SMA20 and SMA50. Higher oil prices are the catalyst this week; macro view is neutral on energy but price action overrides when momentum is this clean.
executed.outcome_notes: The scheduled exit closed the position at exactly the entry price, meaning the oil-driven momentum thesis never converted into realized price movement within the holding window. No directional follow-through developed — either the catalyst was already priced in at entry or the move occurred outside the scheduled hold period.
executed.risks_observed: A flat scheduled exit exposes the strategy's structural dependency on timing: if momentum runs before or after the holding window, the edge is never captured. Single-ETF energy concentration also means any surprise crude inventory print or OPEC headline could have produced an asymmetric loss with no intra-basket offset.

executed.thesis: XLK leads all sectors on 20d relative strength (+14.20%) with macro explicitly bullish on AI capex and semis; elevated volume ratio (1.32) confirms institutional participation. RSI at 73 is stretched so sizing is trimmed and TP widened to let the secular trend run; Friday's rate-fear dip is treated as noise against the dominant uptrend.
executed.outcome_notes: An RSI-73 entry into a rate-fear dip likely stalled the immediate upside, and the scheduled exit locked in a flat result before the secular AI/semis trend could reassert. The trimmed sizing was appropriate given stretch, but the holding window was too short to distinguish noise from a genuine reversal.
executed.risks_observed: Holding both XLE and XLK simultaneously creates a momentum-factor concentration at the basket level — both positions were selected on relative-strength signals, so a broad risk-off rotation would have hit both legs together with no hedge. The RSI-73 entry also left minimal buffer before a stop would be threatened on any intraday volatility spike.
